title: Open Shelf Media Console
summary: A long, low media console with three 1x12 open shelves framed in 2x2 and 1x2 trim — a wide, low-profile stand sized for a TV rather than a general living-room console.
description:
This console is built low and wide specifically for media equipment, with three fully open 1x12 shelves framed front and back in trim rather than a single tabletop over closed storage. Build the top shelf first: pocket holes around all four sides let the 2x2 side trim attach first (inset slightly for a reveal), then the 2x2 front and back trim ties the corners together.

The legs attach to the underside of the completed top next, and **the side trim on the lower shelves sits inset 3/4" from the outside of the legs** — a consistent reveal line worth marking or using a spacer block for, rather than eyeballing it fresh on each shelf. Getting this inset consistent across all three shelves is what makes the finished stack of open shelves read as a single considered design rather than three boards that happen to be stacked.

The remaining shelves attach to the side trim and legs last, each one pocket-holed around all four edges exactly like the top. Because every shelf is fully open, there's no back panel and no hidden storage — this is a display-and-airflow piece, built for a TV, a soundbar, and media boxes.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the top
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes around all four sides of the top shelf board. Drill a single 1-1/2" pocket hole on each end of the 11-1/4" side trim pieces. Attach the side trim with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws and glue, then attach the front and back trim with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws (into the top) and 2-1/2" pocket hole screws (at the corners into the side trim).

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Drill two 1-1/2" pocket holes on one end of each leg. Attach to the underside of the top with glue and 2-1/2" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Add the shelf side trim
steps.body: Inset the side trim 3/4" from the outside of the legs, using a scrap piece of 1x wood as a spacer if helpful. Drill a 3/4" pocket hole on each end of the side trim and attach to the legs with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws and glue.

steps.title: Attach the remaining shelves
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes around all four edges of the shelves, including into the legs. Attach to the side trim and legs with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws and glue.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Low media console with three open 1x12 shelves
